overview: Allergic Rhinitis can cause symptoms seen in primary care. This information is supportive and cannot provide a diagnosis.
self_care: monitor symptoms and keep a simple symptom diary
seek help early if symptoms worsen or do not improve
when_to_see_gp: Contact a GP if symptoms persist, get worse, or are affecting normal daily activities.
when_to_call_111: Call NHS 111 for urgent advice if symptoms are worsening, you are vulnerable, or you are unsure what to do.
when_to_call_999: Call 999 for breathing difficulty, throat swelling, widespread swelling, or collapse.
